title: Chapter Seventy-Five: The Door
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Zhu Lianting had not been sleeping.

Yeon-shin knew this the moment she emerged -- one robe thrown over nightclothes, hair unpinned, energy coiled tight as a drawn bow. She had heard Won-chang's shouting and she had understood exactly what it meant.

She had been planning to visit him at daybreak. To measure his capacity. To determine how much wealth and manpower must be invested for him to fall into her grasp. She had already decided to become a guardian rather than an enemy -- to embrace and control rather than oppose. Making him an enemy was an amateur's method.

But her plans had not accounted for this.

*She came anyway.*

"What crazy --" Vulgar words came from elegant lips. She mastered herself. Stepped outside.

The scene was already a spectacle. Over ten low-ranked Ma family warriors tangled trying to catch Won-chang, who danced through their formation like smoke. His body technique had already surpassed white rank -- flexible and soundless as if he had learned assassin secret techniques.

The divided faction elders did not move. Ma Se-in's guardians could not step forward hastily. Too many eyes watching. Damaging the main family's dignity by crossing hands with a youngster in the main sect? They were trapped by their own propriety.

"Come out," Zhu Lianting said.

The Ma family warriors moved away in unison, military discipline showing in their retreat. There was order in them -- the kind that would shine in group battles.

Then she gathered inner power into her right arm. The blood vessel of the Large Intestine Meridian running from shoulder to index finger burned. The palm technique of the Golden Immortal Eight Methods -- imperial martial arts. They said the technique trained the entire body's meridians, specializing in longevity and defense against sneak attacks. It granted sharp senses able to perceive an opponent's qi and blood in the moment of exchanging a strike.

The power filled her palm in an instant.

BOOM.

Storm-like energy waves gathered in her extending palm. Powerful true qi, extremely precise. A strike to kill a white rank who insulted imperial family with one hit.

She aimed at Won-chang.

Yeon-shin moved.

WHOOSH.

He stepped between them. Smooth. Soundless. The body technique he had refined through countless encounters -- part elf-clan fluidity, part instinct, part the desperation of a body that could not afford to be slow. His vision dyed with blue clothes, Zhu Lianting's eyes widened.

She had missed his approach. His body technique resembling the elf clan's movement was very accomplished.

The distance was too close. Striking Lightning Genius would ruin everything. She desperately tried to withdraw her true qi. Pulled back considerable force in an instant -- enough that Lightning Genius could handle according to rumors.

*Done,* she thought.

But she could not completely withdraw. A trace of power remained. Raising intent and completely controlling true qi simultaneously was a realm matching Desolate Sect black ranks. Her inner power operation had not yet reached that achievement.

THUD.

Her palm struck his solar plexus. The cool sensation of the Desolate Sect blue martial uniform dug into her palm center. And in that instant, the Golden Immortal Eight Methods' sharp senses granted her a full reading of his body.

She felt it.

True qi sprouted from his solar plexus. The middle dantian. His inner power operated with extraordinary precision, gently pressing the Bong-an point at the edge of his lungs. Though it brought blood, organs were not damaged. Extremely fast yet incredibly detailed cultivation.

A once-in-a-lifetime genius. Born with a complete vessel for martial arts.

But she could not admire. In the instant she withdrew her hand, she felt blood rising up his airway. He had deliberately caused the blood vomiting.

*This impudent thing --*

COUGH.

Blood sprayed from Yeon-shin's mouth. He staggered. Collapsed to one knee, then to the ground, hunching over the spreading red on the flagstones. Anyone could see it was because Zhu Lianting had used excessive force.

Her eyebrows raised.

"Please withdraw your hand."

Baek Mi-ryeo stepped forward, ink-black energy wreathing her body in visible tendrils. Energy materialization -- rare cultivation whose origins anyone would find hard to measure. The nature was chilling. Gazes were immediately drawn.

"Demonic arts?" someone in the crowd murmured.

"No. The energy waves are pure."

"Don't you know Bewitching Sword Demon?"

Spectators. Already lined up in front of the Desolate Ma family hall. All Desolate Sect warriors. Those who came running hearing the disturbance numbered dozens.

Cheong Myeong drew his sword before the ink-dark energy could even spread. Hazy wind rose along the blade gripped by the one-eyed noble wearing a hood. Energy waves arose without releasing sword strikes -- an ominous sword wind. He was the Blue Eyed Sword Demon called the Swordsman who could discuss all under heaven in the future. They said his fame was second only to the Radiant Demon Wing leader.

"Step back," was all he said.

And Won-chang, completing the performance with the instinct of a born dramatist, scolded the two seniors with words that contradicted his face: "You two, are you in your right mind? Think of the punishment. That 'Desolate Ma family's Zhu Lianting' won't stay still."

His expression differed from his words. Energy unique to killing sword practitioners gleamed. Internal energy carrying intent created demon-like waves, truly not human in appearance. Anyone would consider it a warrior glaring at a hero's enemies.

He pushed past his seniors and stepped forward.

"THE MURIM'S PRINCIPLES HAVE FALLEN TO THE GROUND!"

True qi gathered in his vocal cords rather than his sword. A voice like Buddhist lion's roar clawing the atmosphere.

"Is this how you treat Radiant Demon Wing's Lightning Genius who made his name through missions? Lawless ones who know no shame! Are you not ashamed before your parents in heaven? Desolate Ma family -- if there are any who would discuss chivalry with this Desolate Sect Hero, step forward!"

His true qi operation that only withdrew force when mentioning parents was tremendous. His entire body emitted powerful spirit.

He knelt beside Yeon-shin. After seeming to examine the solar plexus wound for several breaths, he supported the upper body and began wailing loudly.

"Aigo, aigoo!"

An astounding sight. Grand opera of the kind that flourished in Beijing, performed in a Desolate Sect courtyard at dusk.

Zhu Lianting lowered her hand.

"Parents in heaven?" she echoed coldly.

"Are not imperial family members those who look down on subjects from the azure palace?"

Won-chang's retort was glib. He pulled Yeon-shin's head into his lap and wept -- great, heaving, theatrical sobs.

The spectators pressed closer. The Ma family elders did not move. Could not. Blue-ranked masters among the watchers. Elves who could see through lies. The sight of Lightning Genius crouching on the ground with head deeply bowed was not unsightly -- the young master's bravery in taking the ascending strike for his companion was great.

Zhu Lianting looked down at the scene. At the blood on the flagstones. At the three warriors standing over their fallen companion with death in their eyes.

*She is calculating. Right now, behind that cold face, she is recalculating everything.*

Yeon-shin kept his head down. Blood dripped from his chin. The headache was real -- the crown-point agony that came from pushing his broken body through one more performance. But the blood was his choice. The fall was his design. Won-chang's brilliant, desperate, ridiculous plan, executed perfectly.

*Make it public. Make her the villain. Then give her a way out that serves us both.*

He waited.

---

Zhu Lianting stepped back.

One step. That was all. But in the language of power, it was a retreat. She glanced down and then away.

"Enough," she said quietly. Then, to her warriors: "Bring him inside. See to his injury."

She turned to face the crowd. Her voice carried without effort -- imperial projection, the kind that could silence a throne room.

"Lightning Genius is a son of the Ma family's blood. I was testing him. The excessive force was... regrettable." She paused. "The Desolate Ma family will provide full support for his recovery and future endeavors. He is our own."

Not an apology. A reframing. But a public commitment, witnessed by half the sect.

Yeon-shin allowed himself to be lifted. As Won-chang helped him to his feet, their eyes met. Won-chang's were still red from genuine tears, but the corner of his mouth twitched. An unanticipated event, Zhu Lianting had thought. She was wrong. Every beat of it had been anticipated.

*It worked.*

Inside, in a quiet room smelling of incense and medicine, Zhu Lianting sat across from him. Alone. The door closed.

"You planned this," she said. Not a question.

"I planned to sever ties. My companions had a different idea."

"Crude. Effective. The sort of tactic that works once." She studied him. The sharp senses of her Golden Immortal Eight Methods had given her a reading of his body during the strike. She knew what she had felt -- the precision, the control, the genius of his cultivation. "You are more interesting than I assumed. Not a weapon. Not a pawn. Something else."

"I am a Radiant Demon Wing warrior. That is all I wish to be."

"And what do you want from the Ma family?"

"Nothing. Indifference would be ideal. But since you have publicly claimed me, I will accept the support and stay out of your faction's business. Ma Se-in's succession is secure. I will not contest it. In exchange -- you do not attempt to control my movements, my missions, or my future."

Zhu Lianting was quiet for a long time. Outside, the spectators were dispersing. The night settled.

"You speak as if you have no time for compromise," she said.

Something flickered in his chest. He kept his face still.

"I simply know what I want."

She exhaled. The faintest smile -- the kind that acknowledged a worthy opponent.

"Very well. I will be your guardian in name. You will be our glory in practice. We will see which of us benefits more."

She stood. At the door she paused. Her voice changed -- softer, stripped of imperial projection.

"Your mother's name was Ma Seo-yeon. She was the most talented of her generation. The family's decision to sever ties with her was not unanimous." A beat. "For what it is worth."

She left.

---

Yeon-shin sat in the quiet room. The incense burned. His solar plexus throbbed where her palm had struck. The headache pulsed behind his crown-point, slow and rhythmic, like the ticking of a clock he could not stop.

*Three years. Six months. Minus fifteen days. Minus whatever comes next.*

He thought of Won-chang weeping in that amber room. Of Cheong Myeong's hand on his shoulder -- a single touch, the Elf's way. Of Mi-ryeo's fierce embrace, three heartbeats long. The only three people in the world who knew the truth of what he was, and they had responded not with pity or with plans to use his weakness, but with tears and a scheme and the wordless promise that they would stand between him and whatever came.

He pressed his palm flat against his chest where the blood had been and felt his heart beating.

*Still here.*

He rose. Walked to the door.

When he opened it, the night air hit his face -- cool, clean, carrying the scent of distant rain. The moon hung low and heavy over the Desolate Sect's rooftops, and somewhere in the dark a night bird called once and fell silent.

His three companions waited in the courtyard. Won-chang had stopped crying. Cheong Myeong leaned against a pillar with his arms crossed. Mi-ryeo stood apart, watching the sky.

They looked at him.

He looked back.

No one spoke. The night held them, and the silence was not empty but full -- full of everything that had been said in that amber-lit room, the confession and the grief and the fierce, stubborn refusal to let it be the end.

Yeon-shin walked toward them.

And far beneath the earth, deeper than any dungeon or tomb, in a place where no light had fallen for a thousand years --

the door opened.
